steps.title: Identify the logical relationship between the sentences
steps.content: Sentence 2 makes a broad general claim: the 1991 parade was one of many military parades held in France. Sentence 3 follows with a concrete count — 29 such parades from 1886 to 2022 — that sums up and supports that claim. The transition must signal that sentence 3 is a summary or totaling statement, not a cause, a restatement, or a contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
Test each transition word against that relationship:
  • Choice A — 'Hence,': Signals a conclusion caused by the previous sentence, not a summary total. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In other words,': Signals a restatement in different words, but sentence 3 adds new numeric evidence rather than rephrasing. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Nevertheless,': Signals contrast or concession — no contrast exists here. ✗
  • Choice D — 'In sum,': Signals a summary, fitting the move from a general claim to a concrete total that wraps up the evidence. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
